channel.title: 2024 on Eric Spencer
channel.description: Recent content in 2024 on Eric Spencer

item.title: Family Guy Gag Scraper
item.description: Scripts to scrape and catalog cutaway gags from Family Guy episodes.

item.title: BrightBet.tech
item.description: An AI-powered trade and prediction analysis platform built for HackIllinois 2026.

item.title: Loyola ITS RAG Bot
item.description: A voice-first RAG chatbot trained on Loyola's ITS knowledge base, built after two years of answering the same tickets.

item.title: TLA+ Model of a Walk-In Oven
item.description: A formal TLA+ specification of a walk-in industrial oven with a focus on safety interlocks.

item.title: TLA+ Model of a Laptop
item.description: A formal TLA+ specification modeling a laptop's power states, battery, lid, thermals, and auto-suspend.

item.title: TLA+ Model of Dexcom G7
item.description: A formal TLA+ specification of the Dexcom G7 continuous glucose monitor's behavior and safety properties.

item.title: AI OS
item.description: An experimental AI-centric operating system exploring tight integration between AI and system-level operations.

item.title: COMP322 Final Project Reflection
item.description:
Eric Spencer + Matthew Caballero
December 14, 2024
COMP 322/422 Reflection

Reflections on HealthUp!

Abstract

HealthUp! is an application focused on bettering a user’s information about their health. Users can log nutritional information of foods and track their workouts. We wanted to develop this app to support our passion for healthy living.

Repository (without keys)

GitHub Repository

If you attempt to download and run the app, see the bolded text in “Testing & Iterative Design.” The iOS version of the app does not work.

item.title: Movie Recommendation Website
item.description: A movie recommendation website developed as part of the Loyola AI Club's Fall 2024 project.

item.title: Daily Task - Wellness Tracker
item.description: A habit manager for daily consistency.

item.title: Serenity
item.description: A mental wellness application developed at Northwestern's Wildhacks Hackathon.
